other_observers: Tina Jones, Brenda Linfield; I was not the finder of this bird.
description: I observed the bird through a spotting scope at 20X at a distance of 30 feet, while it was perched.  It was a medium-sized, relatively stocky hummingbird with a greenish back, a grayish forecrown, and a straight, relatively short black bill. The top of the head and the nape were green.  The underparts were a somewhat dingy off-white color, with pale greenish-gold flanks and a triangle-shaped gorget (tapering to a point at the top). I did not see any color in the gorget while the bird was perched, in overcast conditions.  The face showed a bold white spot to the rear of the eye. On close examination, I could see that the spot extended behind, not above the eye.  The forehead had a very gentle slope.Behaviors: flying and feeding like a hummingbird  No behavioral characteristics were used to support my identification to species.Call: none heardPlumage: adult
